policy.goal: Make Claude-dispatched tool requests safe for Codex by checking parity before accepting the task.
never_do: pretend a Claude-only tool is callable from Codex
install paid APIs or metered services without explicit free/no-cost confirmation
create direct-session Claude handoff files unless the user asks

tools.name: firecrawl
tools.next_action: Use Firecrawl CLI if present. If missing, install/configure CLI or route through web-scrape mechanic.

tools.name: printing-press
tools.next_action: Go is required for Printing Press Go CLIs. Install Go or configure a bundled Go path, then install/generate PP CLIs.

tools.name: browser
tools.next_action: Prefer Codex Browser plugin and node_repl browser control. Keep chrome-devtools MCP as Claude-side fallback.

tools.name: github
tools.next_action: Use Codex GitHub plugin when exposed, otherwise GitHub CLI.

tools.name: vercel
tools.next_action: Use Vercel plugin skills and Vercel CLI. Re-auth Vercel MCP if plugin logs invalid_token.

tools.name: supabase
tools.next_action: Use the official npx route (`npx supabase ...`) or install the standalone/Scoop CLI. Do not use unsupported global npm install.

tools.name: obsidian
tools.next_action: Codex can write the local markdown vault directly. MCP parity is optional; do not dump non-durable session logs.

tools.name: gemini
tools.next_action: Codex should use the local Gemini MCP bridge when configured; otherwise hand back Gemini-only long-context tasks.

tools.name: google-workspace
tools.next_action: Use Codex document/spreadsheet plugins for local artifacts. Install Google connectors only when explicitly needed.

tools.name: openai-sites
tools.next_action: OpenAI Sites plugin was not exposed in the checked Codex session. Use @Sites only when the plugin/tool is actually available.
